audit_items.proof_trace: `do_add_delta` calls `do_item_get` at L3265 which calls `refcount_incr`, acquiring a reference that must be released via `do_item_remove` on every non-NULL path. The early-exit path at L3271-3272 (`it->nbytes <= 2 → return NON_NUMERIC`) is the sole path missing this release; every other non-NULL return path (CAS mismatch L3276, strtoull fail L3283, alloc fail L3324, else branch L3341, success L3348) correctly calls `do_item_remove(it)`. The `refcount` field is `unsigned short` (16-bit), so 65535 leaked increments wrap it to 0, enabling a subsequent use-after-free or permanent item pinning that prevents LRU eviction.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Attacker stores a key with a 1-2 byte value (e.g., `set k 0 0 2\r\n\r\n`) then sends repeated `incr k 1` (or binary INCR) commands; each call leaks one refcount unit, permanently pinning the item so it cannot be evicted and exhausting memcached memory (DoS), or wrapping the 16-bit refcount to 0 after 65535 requests to enable use-after-free.
